notes: AMD first desktop 64-bit CPU (Sep 2003, K8 microarchitecture); brought AMD64 ISA to consumer desktops ahead of Intel EM64T
AMD 64-bit extension of x86 (Opteron Apr 2003, Athlon 64 Sep 2003); Intel adopted as Intel 64 (EM64T) 2004; became dominant 64-bit desktop/server ISA
Portable clamshell PCs; thin-and-light category emerged 2003-2005; overtook desktops in unit sales ~2008
Major XP service pack Aug 2004; added Windows Security Center, firewall on by default, NX bit / DEP, Bluetooth stack; fundamental XP security overhaul

tech_name: NX bit / Data Execution Prevention (DEP) / Enhanced Virus Protection
notes: Hardware no-execute page-protection bit; AMD branded "Enhanced Virus Protection"; Intel "XD bit"; OS support in Windows XP SP2 (2004), Linux PaX; blocks code-injection malware

tech_name: Microsoft Windows XP 64-bit Edition / Windows XP Professional x64 Edition
notes: Microsoft 64-bit Windows XP; Itanium edition 2001, AMD64/x64 edition 2005; released in beta Feb 2004; limited driver/application support; displaced by Windows Vista/7 x64
